description:
You have just joined an on-call monitoring team.  
    Your first job is to prepare a dedicated Python virtual environment for a tiny prototype alerting script and then generate two short text artifacts that the rest of the team’s CI pipeline will pull in later.

    1. Create a brand-new venv called “alerts_env” directly under /home/user/ (full path must therefore be “/home/user/alerts_env”).  
       • Use Python’s built-in venv module – no other tools.  
       • Make sure the directory really appears at that exact path when you are finished.

    2. Inside that environment, install *exactly* one package and pin its version:  
          psutil==5.9.5  

       The package version must match 5.9.5 exactly; no other version is acceptable.

    3. Still inside the same activated environment, run one single shell command that lists the packages now present in the environment, filters out the line that starts with “psutil”, and writes ONLY that line to a new log file located at:  
          /home/user/alerts_report.log  

       Output requirements for /home/user/alerts_report.log:  
       • The file must contain exactly one line.  
       • That line must be the standard “pip freeze”-style line for psutil, i.e. it must read precisely:  
             psutil==5.9.5  
         (no leading/trailing spaces, no additional newline lines).

    4. Create an additional short summary file at /home/user/alerts_summary.txt with the following exact three lines (including correct case and the equal signs).  **Nothing else may appear in the file**:  
          ENV_PATH=/home/user/alerts_env  
          PACKAGE=psutil  
          VERSION=5.9.5  

    The whole job can be accomplished with roughly three or four shell commands (creating the venv, activating it, installing the package, producing the two text files).  When you are done, both /home/user/alerts_report.log and /home/user/alerts_summary.txt must exist exactly as specified so that the automated tests can verify them.  Do not move or rename any of the required files or directories.  You do not need to write or run any actual alerting code for this task.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory:
       /home/user/alerts_env
       – standard layout produced by “python -m venv”, e.g. containing “bin/”, “lib/”, “pyvenv.cfg”, etc.

    2. File:
       /home/user/alerts_report.log  
       – one single line, exact content:  
         psutil==5.9.5
       – newline terminator allowed but no extra blank lines.

    3. File:
       /home/user/alerts_summary.txt  
       – exact three-line content (with newline at the end of each line accepted):  
         ENV_PATH=/home/user/alerts_env  
         PACKAGE=psutil  
         VERSION=5.9.5

    Verification logic: 
    • Check that “alerts_env/bin/python” exists and is executable.  
    • Run “/home/user/alerts_env/bin/pip show psutil” and confirm “Version: 5.9.5”.  
    • Read /home/user/alerts_report.log → must be exactly the one expected line.  
    • Read /home/user/alerts_summary.txt → must match the three lines verbatim.
